name: Register new Logon Process by Rubeus
description: Rubeus, a popular open-source tool for Kerberos attacks, is used to register a new logon process. This behavior indicates a potential attempt by an adversary to hijack a legitimate process or service to maintain persistence or evade detection. The detection is based on the behavioral analysis of Rubeus registering a new logon process on a Windows system.

prompts.prompt: Investigate the logon process registration attempt by Rubeus at `{timestamp}` on host `{hostname}`. The command line detected was `{command_line}`. Analyze the user (`{user_name}`) who executed the command and the new logon process that was registered (`{new_logon_process}`). Determine if this is a legitimate administrative action or a malicious attempt to maintain persistence. Provide a summary of the findings and suggest immediate containment and remediation steps.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname where the activity was detected.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line of the process.

parameters.name: user_name
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was executed.

parameters.name: new_logon_process
parameters.description: The name of the new logon process that was registered.
